title: Implement Redirect URL Validation for Salesforce OAuth
acceptance_criteria: OAuth callback validates that the instance_url belongs to salesforce.com domain
Invalid domains redirect to an error page with 'Invalid Salesforce domain' message
Legitimate Salesforce OAuth flows continue to work for all customer subdomains
Validation occurs before any database operations or user creation
middleware_changes: Salesforce OAuth callback controller now validates the instance_url domain before processing. Invalid domains redirect to an error page. Domain validation function checks for salesforce.com in the URL.
implementation_notes: Added isValidSalesforceDomain() method to the OAuth callback controller. The check runs before database operations, validating the response is from salesforce.com. Invalid domains redirect to an invalidRedirectURL route with an error message. Deployed to staging for pen test re-validation.
comment_summary: Product owner initially questioned whether this was a Salesforce-side issue. Developer confirmed it was the application's responsibility and implemented domain validation in the OAuth callback. Changes deployed to staging for penetration test re-validation.
